response:
implement the naive bayes classification algorithm for text classification in python without using any external libraries except for basic ones like math or numpy. the classification will be binary and the input data will be a corpus of text data with corresponding labels. the text data should be preprocessed (remove stop words, stemming, tokenization, etc) and the frequency of each word should be calculated for each class.  

the training function should take in the corpus of labeled texts and calculate the necessary probabilities required by naive bayes. the prediction function should take in a new, unlabeled text and output the predicted label. the evaluation function should use the f1 score to determine the performance of your model.  

the implementation will demonstrate the use of the naive bayes algorithm with a binary classification task. the model will calculate word frequencies for each class and use them to make predictions. the evaluation will show the accuracy of the model based on the f1 score.  

* * *  

the model will process the text data by removing stop words and stemming the words. the frequency of each word will be calculated for each class, allowing the model to make predictions based on the likelihood of words appearing in each class. the prediction function will use the calculated probabilities to determine the most likely class for a new text.  

the evaluation function will compute the f1 score based on the predicted and true labels. the model's performance will be compared to a baseline, and the results will show how well the algorithm performs on the given dataset. the model's accuracy will be discussed in terms of precision, recall, and f1 score.
